examples.name: Basic Example. Describes the fathers death at age 74 from a heart attack

text.div: Father died of a heart attack aged 74

note.text: Was fishing at the time. At least he went doing someting he loved.

examples.name: Mother died from a stroke aged 56. Brother with diabetes.

sections.title: 9.4Resource FamilyMemberHistory - Content
sections.introduction:
Significant health conditions for a person related to the patient relevant in the context of care for the patient.
This resource is referenced by AdverseEvent and ClinicalImpression
sections.Scope and Usage:
FamilyMemberHistory is one of the event resources in the FHIR workflow specification.
This resource records significant health conditions for a particular individual related to the subject. This information can be known to
different levels of accuracy. Sometimes the exact condition ('asthma') is known, and sometimes it is less precise ('some sort of cancer').
Equally, sometimes the person can be identified ('my aunt Agatha') and sometimes all that is known is that the person was an uncle.
This resource represents a simple structure used to capture an 'elementary' family history for a particular family member.  However, it can also be the basis for
capturing a more rigorous history useful for genetic and other analysis - refer to the Genetic Pedigree profile
for an example.
The entire family history for an individual can be represented by combining references to FamilyMemberHistory instances into a List resource instance.

definitions:
FamilyMemberHistory: significant health conditions for a person related to the patient relevant in the context of care for the patient. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.identifier: business identifiers assigned to this family member history by the performer or other systems which remain constant as the resource is updated and propagates from server to server. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.instantiatesCanonical: the url pointing to a fhir-defined protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this familymemberhistory. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.instantiatesUri: the url pointing to an externally maintained protocol, guideline, orderset or other definition that is adhered to in whole or in part by this familymemberhistory. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.status: a code specifying the status of the record of the family history of a specific family member. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.dataAbsentReason: describes why the family member's history is not available. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.patient: the person who this history concerns. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.date: the date (and possibly time) when the family member history was recorded or last updated. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.name: this will either be a name or a description; e.g. "aunt susan", "my cousin with the red hair". [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.relationship: the type of relationship this person has to the patient (father, mother, brother etc.). [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.sex: the birth sex of the family member. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.born[x]: the actual or approximate date of birth of the relative. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.age[x]: the age of the relative at the time the family member history is recorded. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.estimatedAge: if true, indicates that the age value specified is an estimated value. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.deceased[x]: deceased flag or the actual or approximate age of the relative at the time of death for the family member history record. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.reasonCode: describes why the family member history occurred in coded or textual form. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.reasonReference: indicates a condition, observation, allergyintolerance, or questionnaireresponse that justifies this family member history event. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.note: this property allows a non condition-specific note to the made about the related person. ideally, the note would be in the condition property, but this is not always possible. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition: the significant conditions (or condition) that the family member had. this is a repeating section to allow a system to represent more than one condition per resource, though there is nothing stopping multiple resources - one per condition. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition.code: the actual condition specified. could be a coded condition (like mi or diabetes) or a less specific string like 'cancer' depending on how much is known about the condition and the capabilities of the creating system. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition.outcome: indicates what happened following the condition.  if the condition resulted in death, deceased date is captured on the relation. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition.contributedToDeath: this condition contributed to the cause of death of the related person. if contributedtodeath is not populated, then it is unknown. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition.onset[x]: either the age of onset, range of approximate age or descriptive string can be recorded.  for conditions with multiple occurrences, this describes the first known occurrence. [STOP]
FamilyMemberHistory.condition.note: an area where general notes can be placed about this specific condition. [STOP]
